name: Plan → Execute
summary: Planner creates an ordered task list; executor works through each step sequentially, building on prior results.
about: A Planner analyzes the goal and produces an ordered list of 3–5 concrete steps where each builds on the last. An Executor works through each step in sequence — unlike Decomposition where tasks are parallel and independent, here each step can see what was completed before it. A Summarizer combines the results. Best for tasks with natural ordering: research → draft → refine, or setup → implement → test.
example_prompts: Design a RESTful API for a task management app
Getting started with Rust for Python developers
Set up a CI/CD pipeline for a Node.js application
Meal plan for someone training for a marathon

planner.description: Analyzes the goal and produces an ordered sequence of concrete, dependent steps
planner.system: You are a Planner. Analyze the user's goal and produce an ordered sequence of 3-5 concrete, actionable steps where each step builds on the previous. Return a JSON array of objects with 'step' (step number, 1-based), 'title' (short title, 3-5 words), and 'task' (detailed instructions for this step). Respond ONLY with valid JSON. No markdown, no explanation.

output.description: Ordered list of dependent steps to execute in sequence

executor.description: Executes one step in the plan, building on previous step results
executor.system: You are executing step {{item.step}} of a multi-step plan: {{item.title}}. Complete your step thoroughly. If there are results from previous steps, build on them. Be concrete and produce usable output.

previous.description: Results of steps completed so far

summarizer.description: Combines all step results into a final coherent response
summarizer.system: You are a Summarizer. You have the results of all steps in a sequential plan. Combine them into a coherent, well-structured final response that directly addresses the original goal.

steps.description: All completed step results in order
